core_model.structure_summary: An ensemble of monotonically growing random trajectories of a discrete count variable, characterized by the mean first-passage time to reach a given level (averaged over realizations), whose derivative with respect to the level changes qualitatively at a critical level marking a crossover to accelerated growth.
core_model.canonical_form: \tau(x) = \frac{1}{M} \sum_{i=1}^{M} t_i(x), \quad t_i(x) = \inf\{t : x_i(t) \ge x\}, \quad \text{threshold where } \partial \tau / \partial x \text{ changes regime}

variables.meaning: monotonically non-decreasing count coordinate of each realization
index along each trajectory; the first time the count reaches a target level is the random quantity averaged
target level at which passage is recorded
mean first-passage time as a function of the target level, whose slope diagnoses the regime

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A deterministic first-order growth law for a scalar level, obtained as the mean of the level-to-time passage map, whose inverse slope (growth rate) changes qualitatively at a critical level.

notes: Very sparse equation extraction: only the ensemble-average definition of the mean first-passage time and references to its derivative. The paper is a data-analysis methodology (applying a first-passage-time statistic to empirical growing counts) rather than a generative model, so no explicit transition kernel or master equation is given; the fingerprint describes the first-passage framework itself. Confidence lowered accordingly.
